text: A white plate holds crispy cabbage and carrot fritters with a dish of chili sauce.

A wooden cutting board sits on a kitchen counter, accompanied by a carrot, a cabbage, and two empty glass bowls. A hand peels the carrot, collecting the peels on a white plate. The carrot is sliced into thick pieces, then chopped into thin strips and placed in one bowl. The cabbage is halved; one half is shredded, then transferred to the second bowl. The other half is also shredded and added to the bowl, completing the preparation.

On a table, ingredients are arranged: 3/4 cup flour, 1 tsp salt, 1 egg, 3/4 cup water, 3/4 cup chopped cabbage, and 1/2 cup chopped carrots. Hands add flour and salt to a large mixing bowl. An egg is cracked in, followed by water poured gradually while stirring. The mixture becomes a smooth dough. Chopped cabbage and carrots are then mixed into the dough.
"Chopsticks drop cabbage and carrot in oil."

A hand uses chopsticks to place a spoonful of cabbage and carrot mixture into hot oil, frying and bubbling. The fritters are flipped until golden brown, then lifted onto a paper-lined plate to drain excess oil, showing their crispy texture.
A white plate holds crispy cabbage and carrot fritters with chili sauce.
